fullArticle:
For your summer reading pleasure, here is a cross post from The Institutional Risk Analyst. This is one of our most popular interviews this year.  Great book.  -- Chris

In this issue of The Institutional Risk Analyst, we feature a discussion with Mickey M. Maini, founder of Solstice Laboratory and author of The Entropy Trap: What Physics Knows That Markets Don't. Maini spent decades operating across global markets — first as a senior investment banker, then as the CEO of an emerging-market business that scaled from roughly $100 million to over $5 billion in value, and later as a family-office investor and founder of Solstice Laboratory. His framework, developed first as a private instrument to manage capital and now published as The Entropy Trap with a forward by Jim Rickards, treats mark...
